Morning Prayer to Mama Mary

Dear Mama Mary, As I rise to greet this new day, I offer You my thoughts, my words, my actions.

Hold my hand and walk with me, So I may choose kindness over anger, Faith over fear, Peace over worry. Wrap me in your mantle of protection.

Intercede for me to your Son, Jesus, That I may live this day with grace, Love others as He loves me, And glorify God in all I do. Amen 🙏❤

Dito sa Pinas, ubos talaga isang araw mo sa pag pila sa Doctor

Sa US of A, a relative of mine complained na nahihirapan syang huminga. Pinunta sa Urgent Care ng bandang 9am pero past 12noon na sya nacheck ng doctor. Sabi sa kanya hindi equipped ang facility to check on her kaya sinabing magpunta sa Emergency Room ng hospital. Andun sila ng 1pm, na-triage at nag-antay hanggang 6pm para matignan. After, sinabihang antayin consultation with the doctor. Doctor came at 10pm and pinauwi ng 1am. She's 74y/o and hindi sya nakakain from 9am to 1am of next day.

I’m not tall.  And most of the time lagi ako nakatingkayad sa photos namin kasi feel ko nahihirapan sya kumuha nang magandang angle. 

So during our Anniversary, he bought this beautiful light pink dress and unfortunately I was repeatedly tripping over it. So to prevent any accident, he’s always at my side to hold my dress.  I apologized but he said, “you’re my favorite small girl, don’t be sorry”. 

It made my heart so happy. I’m so blessed to have someone who treats me like a Queen 🥹🤍

Maraming tao ang nahihirapan 👍,isa na ang pamilya ko , masakit tingnan ang anak pag nahihirapang huminga😭😭 , parang maka isip ka na ayaw kuna , ang daming tanong sa isipan ko, (but naman lord , my Congenital heart disease , at my syndrome😢😢 pa )kawawa naman ang anak ko, bahala wla kaming pera basta wla lang sakit ang anak ko .
May kaparehas bako dito na nahihirapan huminga pag nakakakita ng mga powderized, pulbos, or yung mga pinong pino like harina, alikabok, o ano basta mga ganun ba. Feeling ko ako lang 😪.

Super guilty parin ako sa nangyari 💔

2 weeks ago na confine ang baby boy ko 9 months old due to GERD. Day 2 namn sa public hospital umuubo sya kaya nalipat kami sa Respi ward for patients na may ubo. 

Day 3 namn may bagong patient na 5 years old boy. His name is Javier and he looks not well talaga. Every 15 to 20 minutes he looks tense na parang he's running out of air(nahihirapan huminga)

Sabi ng watcher nya na ganun talaga kasi may tonsillitis yung bata.

Alam na mismo ng katawan mo kung ano yung totoo bago pa man mangyari ang katotohanan.

Nung abot hanggang tenga na yung tubig, bigla niya akong binitawan, kaya bigla akong lumubog. For context, 4’11” ako at siya ay 5’11” ang height, plus, hindi ako marunong lumangoy or magfloating. Nagpupumiglas ako at nakainom na rin ng tubig, kaya inangkas niya ako sa likod niya. Akala ko ibabalik na niya ako sa gilid ng pool, pero hindi pa man ako nakakarecover para huminga sa pagkakalubog ko, inihagis niya ulit ako sa malalim na parte ng pool. Dahil mas malalim, di ko abot sahig ng pool.

Kahit nandun mga kateam niya, umiyak ako ng umiyak at nagsisigaw kasi nahihirapan ako huminga, at bukod pa dun ay takot ako sa tubig na malalim kaya nilapitan niya ako. Pagkabalik ko sa gilid ng pool ay nanginginig ako sa takot, kaya kinuhanan ako ng kateam niya ng tubig at tinanong ako kung okay lang ba ako at pinagsabihan siya na wag ginagawang biro yung ginawa niya sakin. Habang nanginginig pa rin ako sa takot, bigla niyang sa mga kateam niya, “Pabayaan niyo, di naman totoo yan”, sabay tawa.

Kung hindi sigurado sa uri ng crab - lalo na kung reef crab - Huwag kainin. 

Ang “devil crab/toxic reef crab” ay maaaring magdala ng lason na hindi nawawala sa pagluluto at maaaring magdulot ng pamamanhid, panghihina, at hirap sa paghinga na maaaring ikamatay.

Para sa agarang gabay, tumawag sa 24/7 poison hotlines at sa pinakamalapit na DOH Toxicology Specialty Center / Regional Poison Control Center.

This morning I was reading Mark 15 and remembered something I read here in Threads:

Scholars say sexual abuse was a common humiliation method used by the Roman soldiers on people about to be crucified. I looked it up and yes there are records of this. 

We know a man who was crucified for us. He was spit on, stripped naked, punched. Is it possible that he was also a sexual abuse victim? My heart breaks and my mind can’t process this!! I’m like, “Hindi naman nangyari sayo yon Lord diba??” 😭

I can’t fully grasp the extent of Jesus’ sacrifice. Maduraan pa nga lang parang grabe na. Pero sinuntok, hinampas, hinubaran din sya. And now this?? 💔 

Ito lang ang nasabi ko after this realization: 

For all that Jesus did for me on the trial and the Cross, I want to live a life worthy of Him. 

I will always try to be a godly wife, a good daughter and sister, and a faithful disciple. Because I literally don’t have anything else to give back to Him. 😭💔

Handa ka bang maghintay kahit walang kasiguraduhan?
Kahit walang clear na timeline, walang solid na promise—hope lang ang hawak mo?

At first, waiting feels romantic. Parang loyalty. Parang devotion. Parang pinapakita mo kung gaano ka ka-deep magmahal. Bawat araw na nag-i-stay ka feels like proof na “ako, hindi ako sumusuko.”

Pero habang tumatagal, nag-iiba ang bigat.  1/5

Yung waiting nagiging uncertainty.
Nagiging overthinking ng 2AM.
Nagiging tahimik na tanong sa dibdib mo na paulit-ulit bumubulong, “Hanggang kailan ba talaga?”

Hindi mo na alam kung strong ka pa ba…
o unti-unti ka nang nauubos.

Love can make you patient.
Love can make you believe in “soon” and “someday.”
Pero hindi pwedeng ikaw lang ang naniniwala. Hindi pwedeng ikaw lang ang kumakapit.

Kasi minsan, waiting is love.
Pero minsan din, waiting is self-abandonment. 2/5

Ako yung laging nakikinig
sa kwento ng iba,
umaalalay kapag mabigat ang gabi,
nagpapatawa kapag umiiyak ang mundo nila.

Ako yung nagsasabing,
“Hindi ka nag-iisa.”

Pero pag ako na ang tahimik,
walang nakakapansin.
Kapag ako na ang napapagod,
walang nagtatanong kung kaya ko pa ba.

Sanay akong maging sandalan,
kaya hindi nila naisip
na minsan
gusto ko ring may masandalan.

Siguro ganito talaga—
ang taong marunong magparamdam 
ng presensya
madalas hindi hinahanap.

-Water

- plastic or ziplock for your phone or anything na important 
-guide
-hiking shoes/sandal if wala running shoes is fine but hinay² kay naay uban trail dangug
-rest and proper warm up
-sweets snacks

-sunscreen

There was a time I was in such grief, I went and seen my Great Grandmother at her gravesite, While I was cleansing her headstone and my Great Aunt headstone, I heard a voice that told me to come sit with them. I looked over at an elderly woman motioning me. I sat with her talking about my things to get advice, I got prompted to clean the headstone in front of me. Not saying much, she said what I was using smelled good (I made the cleanser).

I felt myself get lighter and happier, then the elder told me to take care of my heart. She looked like she had to be every bit of 80+ but active, slightly bent over. I heard her say “you’ll be alright” and felt like a hand was on my shoulder. I looked up and nobody was there, I was out there by myself and a lady bug was on the headstone of the stranger’s headstone that I cleaned. (The type of ways I’ve encountered spirits, I tell ya)

As a GI oncologist, I get excited about good science, but I’m careful about overinterpreting it so let’s talk about this new headline! 

The real takeaway here is not “virus causes cancer.”

It’s that strain-level microbiome differences may distinguish colorectal cancer states.

That’s biologically intriguing. It could inform future biomarker development. But this was largely cross-sectional data. Timing is unclear. Causality is unproven.

In oncology, interesting does not equal actionable.
